title: lonchocarpus obovatus
intro: Lonchocarpus obovatus, also known as the Baboon Tree or African Keelwood, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its extracts have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The Lonchocarpus obovatus is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally or applied topically, depending on the desired health benefit.
